Bomb is a dark comedy about Dasha, a Ukrainian activist whose brain won’t shut up. The year is 2017. Dasha is trying to save the world, fix her relationship, and get her therapist to stop asking questions she doesn’t want to answer. It’s not going well. Her thoughts are spiraling, her reality is bending, and every conversation feels like a small explosion. As the pressure builds, Dasha starts to unravel... Bomb is fast, funny, darkly absurd, and a little bit tragic. It’s about burnout, big emotions, and staying sane when the world feels like it’s blowing up.

601:42204.showDesc:
Karin Fekko weaves tales of hilarity and tragedy through 17 years of keeping her MS a secret while partying, dating, and trying to land her dream job. This show is about invisible disability, internalized ableism, and big-time denial. Can we laugh about it? Karin thinks so. Join her as she marks 20 years since her MS diagnosis.

Website/Socials: @karinfekko

Multiple Neurosis is a storytelling show about a disease I have called Multiple Sclerosis. I tell stories about having a disability that is invisible. For example, when I was blind in one eye, other people did not notice. I talk about hiding my disability for 17 years, when going to parties, going on dates, and going to work. The show is about me trying to feel ok about having a disability, and not being ashamed. I have had Multiple Sclerosis for 20 years, and I finally tell people about it, instead of hiding it. I balance the serious parts of the show with fun and comedy.

601:43041.showDesc:
Spookily accurate (if bonkers) audience readings, songs & 70s team games. The Singing Psychic reveals the songs in your heart. Edinburgh & Adelaide Fringe sell-out show from the UK. Best Comedy Show Finalist (MontrealFringe) "A gloriously entertaining night out”(FemaleArts) 'Can work a crowd like no other' (GrumpyGayCritic) ★★★★

Playwright: Marysia Trembecka
Cast: Marysia Trembecka
Designers: Martin Butterworth

https://www.singingpsychic.tv marysiatrembecka.com | @TheSingingPsychic, @marysiatrembecka | X: @MarysiaT | YT: TheSingingPsychic

Do you possess hidden psychic powers waiting to be unveiled? Prepare to discover your inner mystic—direct from London England, The Singing Psychic promises an evening brimming with glittering 70s-style games, toe-tapping tunes and prizes. The Singing Psychic Game Show has captivated audiences worldwide with its blend of comedy and uncanny accuracy. From revealing the melodies in your heart to dispensing relationship advice, probing life's deepest questions and inspiring audiences, The Singing Psychic leaves no stone unturned in her quest to entertain and enlighten. The biggest attraction is her spookily accurate (if utterly bonkers) audience readings. Theatregoers can expect a whirlwind of entertainment with a line-up of fabulous team games including psychic Jenga, psychic bingo, audience readings, freeze dance, for‘tunes’ and more. Each show is a unique experience fuelled by audience interaction and improvised, heart-warming hilarity. Whether arriving solo or with friends, there's a place for everyone in The Singing Psychic's realm of fun and mystery. As The Singing Psychic says “I have just two rules – one is that what I reveal in the room stays in the room, and the other is that I am ALWAYS right.” The Singing Psychic Game Show is brilliantly bananas. It’s an hour of fun that builds on three years of sell-out Edinburgh and Adelaide Fringe shows and four and five star Edinburgh Fringe reviews; a Funny Women Best Show nomination, two Montreal Fringe nominations (Prix de Creativite, Outstanding Comedy Show); a European tour; and a special appearance at the official BRIT Awards after- party. The Singing Psychic is also a TEDx speaker.

601:43715.showName: SHIRLEY GNOME: Children, You Can't Beat 'Em! (this is not a kid's show)
601:43715.showDesc:
Juno nominee and last year’s holdover Shirley Gnome returns with a new direction. After accidentally creating a viral kids’ song on TikTok, the R-rated singer tries to cash in with a children’s music hour. Spoiler: it doesn’t go well. ★★★★★ “a bona fide genius” -Edmonton Journal. NOT SUITABLE FOR KIDS.

Playwright: Shirley Gnome, Becky Johnson
Cast: Shirley Gnome, children who wish to remain anonymous

Shirley Gnome is a singer who has spent sixteen years performing songs about adult topics. Even though she’s been doing this for a long time, she’s never had a big hit. That changes when a video of her at a music festival, looking a bit "out of it", gets posted on TikTok by a teenager. The video quickly goes viral. A popular TikTok creator named DJ BBN0$e turns the video into a song that becomes a huge hit with kids — like the next “Baby Shark.” Suddenly, Shirley finds herself famous with a new, younger audience. She decides to go along with it to make more money. She’s invited to perform at an elite private school, hoping this new direction will finally pay off. But things quickly fall apart. The kids hate the songs, and her lyrics raise disturbing questions that no child (or parent) is ready for. Through this experience, Shirley starts to reflect on her fears about the world, the future, and the kind of planet we’re leaving behind. What starts as a cash grab gig turns into something deeper — a search for hope. Let’s be clear: this is not a real kids' show. It’s satire. Please do not bring your children. Seriously.

Satire

Kiki Moritsugu has a solo show where she talks about her strong connection with her mum, Jo Hutchings, who was a performer. In the show, Kiki sings, dances, and tells stories. She explains how growing up with a mum who was so bold and different helped her figure out who she is. Kiki is mixed race (part Japanese-Canadian and part English-Canadian), and she shares behind-the-scenes moments from her own job. She talks about how hard it was to find her own voice when other people had ideas about who she should be. The songs in the show are like a soundtrack to Kiki's life, helping us understand how she learned about herself. Now that Kiki has two kids who are also performers, the show ends with her feeling sure of herself and ready to make the future she wants for them.

He’s the music director behind big shows like Frozen and A Christmas Carol at the Citadel Theatre. Life can surprise us. One moment brings joy, the next brings heartbreak. 601:43716.showDesc:
Juno nominee and last year’s holdover Shirley Gnome returns with a new direction. After accidentally creating a viral kids’ song on TikTok, the R-rated singer tries to cash in with a children’s music hour. Spoiler: it doesn’t go well. ★★★★★ “a bona fide genius” -Edmonton Journal. NOT SUITABLE FOR KIDS.

Playwright: Shirley Gnome, Becky Johnson
Cast: Shirley Gnome, children who wish to remain anonymous

Shirley Gnome is a singer who has spent sixteen years performing songs about adult topics. Even though she’s been doing this for a long time, she’s never had a big hit. That changes when a video of her at a music festival, looking a bit "out of it", gets posted on TikTok by a teenager. The video quickly goes viral. A popular TikTok creator named DJ BBN0$e turns the video into a song that becomes a huge hit with kids — like the next “Baby Shark.” Suddenly, Shirley finds herself famous with a new, younger audience. She decides to go along with it to make more money. She’s invited to perform at an elite private school, hoping this new direction will finally pay off. But things quickly fall apart. The kids hate the songs, and her lyrics raise disturbing questions that no child (or parent) is ready for. Through this experience, Shirley starts to reflect on her fears about the world, the future, and the kind of planet we’re leaving behind. What starts as a cash grab gig turns into something deeper — a search for hope. Let’s be clear: this is not a real kids' show. It’s satire. Please do not bring your children. Seriously.

Multiple Neurosis is a storytelling show about a disease I have called Multiple Sclerosis. I tell stories about having a disability that is invisible. For example, when I was blind in one eye, other people did not notice. I talk about hiding my disability for 17 years, when going to parties, going on dates, and going to work. The show is about me trying to feel ok about having a disability, and not being ashamed. I have had Multiple Sclerosis for 20 years, and I finally tell people about it, instead of hiding it. I balance the serious parts of the show with fun and comedy.

Storytelling

In university, he meets Jenny, who likes him even though he’s unsure of himself. Scared of getting close, Keith tells her a lie outside a KFC. That lie forces him to rethink everything, including his role models, and helps him see the good influences that shaped who he really is.

601:43042.showDesc:
Spookily accurate (if bonkers) audience readings, songs & 70s team games. The Singing Psychic reveals the songs in your heart. Edinburgh & Adelaide Fringe sell-out show from the UK. Best Comedy Show Finalist (MontrealFringe) "A gloriously entertaining night out”(FemaleArts) 'Can work a crowd like no other' (GrumpyGayCritic) ★★★★

Playwright: Marysia Trembecka
Cast: Marysia Trembecka

https://www.singingpsychic.tv marysiatrembecka.com | @TheSingingPsychic, @marysiatrembecka | X: @MarysiaT | YT: TheSingingPsychic

Do you possess hidden psychic powers waiting to be unveiled? Prepare to discover your inner mystic—direct from London England, The Singing Psychic promises an evening brimming with glittering 70s-style games, toe-tapping tunes and prizes. The Singing Psychic Game Show has captivated audiences worldwide with its blend of comedy and uncanny accuracy. From revealing the melodies in your heart to dispensing relationship advice, probing life's deepest questions and inspiring audiences, The Singing Psychic leaves no stone unturned in her quest to entertain and enlighten. The biggest attraction is her spookily accurate (if utterly bonkers) audience readings. Theatregoers can expect a whirlwind of entertainment with a line-up of fabulous team games including psychic Jenga, psychic bingo, audience readings, freeze dance, for‘tunes’ and more. Each show is a unique experience fuelled by audience interaction and improvised, heart-warming hilarity. Whether arriving solo or with friends, there's a place for everyone in The Singing Psychic's realm of fun and mystery. As The Singing Psychic says “I have just two rules – one is that what I reveal in the room stays in the room, and the other is that I am ALWAYS right.” The Singing Psychic Game Show is brilliantly bananas. It’s an hour of fun that builds on three years of sell-out Edinburgh and Adelaide Fringe shows and four and five star Edinburgh Fringe reviews; a Funny Women Best Show nomination, two Montreal Fringe nominations (Prix de Creativite, Outstanding Comedy Show); a European tour; and a special appearance at the official BRIT Awards after- party. The Singing Psychic is also a TEDx speaker.

Mathew’s Big Broadway Bash is a fun and feel-good cabaret show filled with songs from your favourite musicals. It’s not a big Broadway show with big money — it’s a small, heartwarming show made with love, creativity, and a lot of passion. You’ll hear songs from shows like Grease, Cats, Mamma Mia!, The Little Mermaid, Pippin, and The Sound of Music. This is more than just a show — it’s a celebration! Kiki Moritsugu holds forth on her relationship with her larger-than-life mother. Sharing three generations of backstage stories from mall shows in Canada to Broadway openings, Kiki reveals how one multi-racial woman defines herself in a world dominated by stereotypes. A Woman Is... a cabaret about becoming who you are.

Playwright: Danielle Dresden & Kiki Moritsugu
Director: Francisco Torres
Cast: Kiki Moritsugu
Designers: Lyn Pilch (Choreography) Lizzie Haller (Music Direction)
Stage Manager: Ingrid Klottke
Accompianist: Mya Rosario

https://FB: Kiki Moritsugu | IG: @kiki_moritsugu

Kiki Moritsugu has a solo show where she talks about her strong connection with her mum, Jo Hutchings, who was a performer. In the show, Kiki sings, dances, and tells stories. She explains how growing up with a mum who was so bold and different helped her figure out who she is. Kiki is mixed race (part Japanese-Canadian and part English-Canadian), and she shares behind-the-scenes moments from her own job. She talks about how hard it was to find her own voice when other people had ideas about who she should be. The songs in the show are like a soundtrack to Kiki's life, helping us understand how she learned about herself. Now that Kiki has two kids who are also performers, the show ends with her feeling sure of herself and ready to make the future she wants for them.

601:43043.showDesc:
Spookily accurate (if bonkers) audience readings, songs & 70s team games. The Singing Psychic reveals the songs in your heart. Edinburgh & Adelaide Fringe sell-out show from the UK. Best Comedy Show Finalist (MontrealFringe) "A gloriously entertaining night out”(FemaleArts) 'Can work a crowd like no other' (GrumpyGayCritic) ★★★★

https://www.singingpsychic.tv marysiatrembecka.com | @TheSingingPsychic, @marysiatrembecka | X: @MarysiaT | YT: TheSingingPsychic

Do you possess hidden psychic powers waiting to be unveiled? Prepare to discover your inner mystic—direct from London England, The Singing Psychic promises an evening brimming with glittering 70s-style games, toe-tapping tunes and prizes. The Singing Psychic Game Show has captivated audiences worldwide with its blend of comedy and uncanny accuracy. From revealing the melodies in your heart to dispensing relationship advice, probing life's deepest questions and inspiring audiences, The Singing Psychic leaves no stone unturned in her quest to entertain and enlighten. The biggest attraction is her spookily accurate (if utterly bonkers) audience readings. Theatregoers can expect a whirlwind of entertainment with a line-up of fabulous team games including psychic Jenga, psychic bingo, audience readings, freeze dance, for‘tunes’ and more. Each show is a unique experience fuelled by audience interaction and improvised, heart-warming hilarity. Whether arriving solo or with friends, there's a place for everyone in The Singing Psychic's realm of fun and mystery. As The Singing Psychic says “I have just two rules – one is that what I reveal in the room stays in the room, and the other is that I am ALWAYS right.” The Singing Psychic Game Show is brilliantly bananas. It’s an hour of fun that builds on three years of sell-out Edinburgh and Adelaide Fringe shows and four and five star Edinburgh Fringe reviews; a Funny Women Best Show nomination, two Montreal Fringe nominations (Prix de Creativite, Outstanding Comedy Show); a European tour; and a special appearance at the official BRIT Awards after- party. The Singing Psychic is also a TEDx speaker.

601:43717.showName: SHIRLEY GNOME: Children, You Can't Beat 'Em! (this is not a kid's show)
601:43717.showDesc:
Juno nominee and last year’s holdover Shirley Gnome returns with a new direction. After accidentally creating a viral kids’ song on TikTok, the R-rated singer tries to cash in with a children’s music hour. Spoiler: it doesn’t go well. ★★★★★ “a bona fide genius” -Edmonton Journal. NOT SUITABLE FOR KIDS.

Playwright: Shirley Gnome, Becky Johnson
Cast: Shirley Gnome, children who wish to remain anonymous

Shirley Gnome is a singer who has spent sixteen years performing songs about adult topics. Even though she’s been doing this for a long time, she’s never had a big hit. That changes when a video of her at a music festival, looking a bit "out of it", gets posted on TikTok by a teenager. The video quickly goes viral. A popular TikTok creator named DJ BBN0$e turns the video into a song that becomes a huge hit with kids — like the next “Baby Shark.” Suddenly, Shirley finds herself famous with a new, younger audience. She decides to go along with it to make more money. She’s invited to perform at an elite private school, hoping this new direction will finally pay off. But things quickly fall apart. The kids hate the songs, and her lyrics raise disturbing questions that no child (or parent) is ready for. Through this experience, Shirley starts to reflect on her fears about the world, the future, and the kind of planet we’re leaving behind. What starts as a cash grab gig turns into something deeper — a search for hope. Let’s be clear: this is not a real kids' show. It’s satire. Please do not bring your children. Seriously.

How To Pack A Revolution In Your Suitcase is about artists in 1920s Germany who made new kinds of music, writing, and art. When the Nazis came to power in 1933, they tried to destroy these artists and their work. Many artists were hurt or jailed, but some got away and took their ideas with them around the world. Their art helped change the world. Bremner tells their stories and sings songs from that time, showing how their problems are like the ones we face today.

601:42206.showDesc:
Karin Fekko weaves tales of hilarity and tragedy through 17 years of keeping her MS a secret while partying, dating, and trying to land her dream job. This show is about invisible disability, internalized ableism, and big-time denial. Can we laugh about it? Karin thinks so. Join her as she marks 20 years since her MS diagnosis.

Website/Socials: @karinfekko

Multiple Neurosis is a storytelling show about a disease I have called Multiple Sclerosis. I tell stories about having a disability that is invisible. For example, when I was blind in one eye, other people did not notice. I talk about hiding my disability for 17 years, when going to parties, going on dates, and going to work. The show is about me trying to feel ok about having a disability, and not being ashamed. I have had Multiple Sclerosis for 20 years, and I finally tell people about it, instead of hiding it. I balance the serious parts of the show with fun and comedy.

601:42922.showDesc:
Kiki Moritsugu holds forth on her relationship with her larger-than-life mother. Sharing three generations of backstage stories from mall shows in Canada to Broadway openings, Kiki reveals how one multi-racial woman defines herself in a world dominated by stereotypes. A Woman Is... a cabaret about becoming who you are.

Playwright: Danielle Dresden & Kiki Moritsugu
Director: Francisco Torres
Cast: Kiki Moritsugu
Designers: Lyn Pilch (Choreography) Lizzie Haller (Music Direction)
Stage Manager: Ingrid Klottke
Accompianist: Mya Rosario

https://FB: Kiki Moritsugu | IG: @kiki_moritsugu

Kiki Moritsugu has a solo show where she talks about her strong connection with her mum, Jo Hutchings, who was a performer. In the show, Kiki sings, dances, and tells stories. She explains how growing up with a mum who was so bold and different helped her figure out who she is. Kiki is mixed race (part Japanese-Canadian and part English-Canadian), and she shares behind-the-scenes moments from her own job. She talks about how hard it was to find her own voice when other people had ideas about who she should be. The songs in the show are like a soundtrack to Kiki's life, helping us understand how she learned about herself. Now that Kiki has two kids who are also performers, the show ends with her feeling sure of herself and ready to make the future she wants for them.

601:43718.showDesc:
Juno nominee and last year’s holdover Shirley Gnome returns with a new direction. After accidentally creating a viral kids’ song on TikTok, the R-rated singer tries to cash in with a children’s music hour. Spoiler: it doesn’t go well. ★★★★★ “a bona fide genius” -Edmonton Journal. NOT SUITABLE FOR KIDS.

Playwright: Shirley Gnome, Becky Johnson
Cast: Shirley Gnome, children who wish to remain anonymous

Shirley Gnome is a singer who has spent sixteen years performing songs about adult topics. Even though she’s been doing this for a long time, she’s never had a big hit. That changes when a video of her at a music festival, looking a bit "out of it", gets posted on TikTok by a teenager. The video quickly goes viral. A popular TikTok creator named DJ BBN0$e turns the video into a song that becomes a huge hit with kids — like the next “Baby Shark.” Suddenly, Shirley finds herself famous with a new, younger audience. She decides to go along with it to make more money. She’s invited to perform at an elite private school, hoping this new direction will finally pay off. But things quickly fall apart. The kids hate the songs, and her lyrics raise disturbing questions that no child (or parent) is ready for. Through this experience, Shirley starts to reflect on her fears about the world, the future, and the kind of planet we’re leaving behind. What starts as a cash grab gig turns into something deeper — a search for hope. Let’s be clear: this is not a real kids' show. It’s satire. Please do not bring your children. Seriously.

601:42923.showDesc:
Kiki Moritsugu holds forth on her relationship with her larger-than-life mother. Sharing three generations of backstage stories from mall shows in Canada to Broadway openings, Kiki reveals how one multi-racial woman defines herself in a world dominated by stereotypes. A Woman Is... a cabaret about becoming who you are.

Playwright: Danielle Dresden & Kiki Moritsugu
Director: Francisco Torres
Cast: Kiki Moritsugu
Designers: Lyn Pilch (Choreography) Lizzie Haller (Music Direction)
Stage Manager: Ingrid Klottke
Accompianist: Mya Rosario

https://FB: Kiki Moritsugu | IG: @kiki_moritsugu

Kiki Moritsugu has a solo show where she talks about her strong connection with her mum, Jo Hutchings, who was a performer. In the show, Kiki sings, dances, and tells stories. She explains how growing up with a mum who was so bold and different helped her figure out who she is. Kiki is mixed race (part Japanese-Canadian and part English-Canadian), and she shares behind-the-scenes moments from her own job. She talks about how hard it was to find her own voice when other people had ideas about who she should be. The songs in the show are like a soundtrack to Kiki's life, helping us understand how she learned about herself. Now that Kiki has two kids who are also performers, the show ends with her feeling sure of herself and ready to make the future she wants for them.

601:43044.showDesc:
Spookily accurate (if bonkers) audience readings, songs & 70s team games. The Singing Psychic reveals the songs in your heart. Edinburgh & Adelaide Fringe sell-out show from the UK. Best Comedy Show Finalist (MontrealFringe) "A gloriously entertaining night out”(FemaleArts) 'Can work a crowd like no other' (GrumpyGayCritic) ★★★★

Playwright: Marysia Trembecka
Cast: Marysia Trembecka
Designers: Martin Butterworth

https://www.singingpsychic.tv marysiatrembecka.com | @TheSingingPsychic, @marysiatrembecka | X: @MarysiaT | YT: TheSingingPsychic

Do you possess hidden psychic powers waiting to be unveiled? Prepare to discover your inner mystic—direct from London England, The Singing Psychic promises an evening brimming with glittering 70s-style games, toe-tapping tunes and prizes. The Singing Psychic Game Show has captivated audiences worldwide with its blend of comedy and uncanny accuracy. From revealing the melodies in your heart to dispensing relationship advice, probing life's deepest questions and inspiring audiences, The Singing Psychic leaves no stone unturned in her quest to entertain and enlighten. The biggest attraction is her spookily accurate (if utterly bonkers) audience readings. Theatregoers can expect a whirlwind of entertainment with a line-up of fabulous team games including psychic Jenga, psychic bingo, audience readings, freeze dance, for‘tunes’ and more. Each show is a unique experience fuelled by audience interaction and improvised, heart-warming hilarity. Whether arriving solo or with friends, there's a place for everyone in The Singing Psychic's realm of fun and mystery. As The Singing Psychic says “I have just two rules – one is that what I reveal in the room stays in the room, and the other is that I am ALWAYS right.” The Singing Psychic Game Show is brilliantly bananas. It’s an hour of fun that builds on three years of sell-out Edinburgh and Adelaide Fringe shows and four and five star Edinburgh Fringe reviews; a Funny Women Best Show nomination, two Montreal Fringe nominations (Prix de Creativite, Outstanding Comedy Show); a European tour; and a special appearance at the official BRIT Awards after- party. The Singing Psychic is also a TEDx speaker.

601:43994.showDesc:
Juno nominee and last year’s holdover Shirley Gnome returns with a new direction. After accidentally creating a viral kids’ song on TikTok, the R-rated singer tries to cash in with a children’s music hour. Spoiler: it doesn’t go well. ★★★★★ “a bona fide genius” -Edmonton Journal. NOT SUITABLE FOR KIDS.

Playwright: Shirley Gnome, Becky Johnson
Cast: Shirley Gnome, children who wish to remain anonymous

Shirley Gnome is a singer who has spent sixteen years performing songs about adult topics. Even though she’s been doing this for a long time, she’s never had a big hit. That changes when a video of her at a music festival, looking a bit "out of it", gets posted on TikTok by a teenager. The video quickly goes viral. A popular TikTok creator named DJ BBN0$e turns the video into a song that becomes a huge hit with kids — like the next “Baby Shark.” Suddenly, Shirley finds herself famous with a new, younger audience. She decides to go along with it to make more money. She’s invited to perform at an elite private school, hoping this new direction will finally pay off. But things quickly fall apart. The kids hate the songs, and her lyrics raise disturbing questions that no child (or parent) is ready for. Through this experience, Shirley starts to reflect on her fears about the world, the future, and the kind of planet we’re leaving behind. What starts as a cash grab gig turns into something deeper — a search for hope. Let’s be clear: this is not a real kids' show. It’s satire. Please do not bring your children. Seriously.

Karin Fekko weaves tales of hilarity and tragedy through 17 years of keeping her MS a secret while partying, dating, and trying to land her dream job. This show is about invisible disability, internalized ableism, and big-time denial. Can we laugh about it? Karin thinks so. Join her as she marks 20 years since her MS diagnosis.

Website/Socials: @karinfekko

Multiple Neurosis is a storytelling show about a disease I have called Multiple Sclerosis. I tell stories about having a disability that is invisible. For example, when I was blind in one eye, other people did not notice. I talk about hiding my disability for 17 years, when going to parties, going on dates, and going to work. The show is about me trying to feel ok about having a disability, and not being ashamed. I have had Multiple Sclerosis for 20 years, and I finally tell people about it, instead of hiding it. I balance the serious parts of the show with fun and comedy.

Growing up Gen X isn’t easy. Especially if you’re a Muslim girl in a small BC town, trying to keep it a secret. This one-woman show follows a funny but struggling narrator as she deals with racism, identity, trauma, and being a teenager in the 1980s. Part stand-up, part theatre, it’s a personal and honest story about fighting to be yourself in a world that wants you to fit in. 601:42924.showDesc:
Kiki Moritsugu holds forth on her relationship with her larger-than-life mother. Sharing three generations of backstage stories from mall shows in Canada to Broadway openings, Kiki reveals how one multi-racial woman defines herself in a world dominated by stereotypes. A Woman Is... a cabaret about becoming who you are.

Playwright: Danielle Dresden & Kiki Moritsugu
Director: Francisco Torres
Cast: Kiki Moritsugu
Designers: Lyn Pilch (Choreography) Lizzie Haller (Music Direction)
Stage Manager: Ingrid Klottke
Accompianist: Mya Rosario

https://FB: Kiki Moritsugu | IG: @kiki_moritsugu

Kiki Moritsugu has a solo show where she talks about her strong connection with her mum, Jo Hutchings, who was a performer. In the show, Kiki sings, dances, and tells stories. She explains how growing up with a mum who was so bold and different helped her figure out who she is. Kiki is mixed race (part Japanese-Canadian and part English-Canadian), and she shares behind-the-scenes moments from her own job. She talks about how hard it was to find her own voice when other people had ideas about who she should be. The songs in the show are like a soundtrack to Kiki's life, helping us understand how she learned about herself. Now that Kiki has two kids who are also performers, the show ends with her feeling sure of herself and ready to make the future she wants for them.

601:43045.showDesc:
Spookily accurate (if bonkers) audience readings, songs & 70s team games. The Singing Psychic reveals the songs in your heart. Edinburgh & Adelaide Fringe sell-out show from the UK. Best Comedy Show Finalist (MontrealFringe) "A gloriously entertaining night out”(FemaleArts) 'Can work a crowd like no other' (GrumpyGayCritic) ★★★★

Playwright: Marysia Trembecka
Cast: Marysia Trembecka
Designers: Martin Butterworth

https://www.singingpsychic.tv marysiatrembecka.com | @TheSingingPsychic, @marysiatrembecka | X: @MarysiaT | YT: TheSingingPsychic

Do you possess hidden psychic powers waiting to be unveiled? Prepare to discover your inner mystic—direct from London England, The Singing Psychic promises an evening brimming with glittering 70s-style games, toe-tapping tunes and prizes. The Singing Psychic Game Show has captivated audiences worldwide with its blend of comedy and uncanny accuracy. From revealing the melodies in your heart to dispensing relationship advice, probing life's deepest questions and inspiring audiences, The Singing Psychic leaves no stone unturned in her quest to entertain and enlighten. The biggest attraction is her spookily accurate (if utterly bonkers) audience readings. Theatregoers can expect a whirlwind of entertainment with a line-up of fabulous team games including psychic Jenga, psychic bingo, audience readings, freeze dance, for‘tunes’ and more. Each show is a unique experience fuelled by audience interaction and improvised, heart-warming hilarity. Whether arriving solo or with friends, there's a place for everyone in The Singing Psychic's realm of fun and mystery. As The Singing Psychic says “I have just two rules – one is that what I reveal in the room stays in the room, and the other is that I am ALWAYS right.” The Singing Psychic Game Show is brilliantly bananas. It’s an hour of fun that builds on three years of sell-out Edinburgh and Adelaide Fringe shows and four and five star Edinburgh Fringe reviews; a Funny Women Best Show nomination, two Montreal Fringe nominations (Prix de Creativite, Outstanding Comedy Show); a European tour; and a special appearance at the official BRIT Awards after- party. The Singing Psychic is also a TEDx speaker.

Shirley Gnome is a singer who has spent sixteen years performing songs about adult topics. Even though she’s been doing this for a long time, she’s never had a big hit. That changes when a video of her at a music festival, looking a bit "out of it", gets posted on TikTok by a teenager. The video quickly goes viral. A popular TikTok creator named DJ BBN0$e turns the video into a song that becomes a huge hit with kids — like the next “Baby Shark.” Suddenly, Shirley finds herself famous with a new, younger audience. She decides to go along with it to make more money. She’s invited to perform at an elite private school, hoping this new direction will finally pay off. But things quickly fall apart. The kids hate the songs, and her lyrics raise disturbing questions that no child (or parent) is ready for. Through this experience, Shirley starts to reflect on her fears about the world, the future, and the kind of planet we’re leaving behind. What starts as a cash grab gig turns into something deeper — a search for hope. Let’s be clear: this is not a real kids' show. It’s satire. Please do not bring your children. Seriously.

Satire

Kiki Moritsugu has a solo show where she talks about her strong connection with her mum, Jo Hutchings, who was a performer. In the show, Kiki sings, dances, and tells stories. She explains how growing up with a mum who was so bold and different helped her figure out who she is. Kiki is mixed race (part Japanese-Canadian and part English-Canadian), and she shares behind-the-scenes moments from her own job. She talks about how hard it was to find her own voice when other people had ideas about who she should be. The songs in the show are like a soundtrack to Kiki's life, helping us understand how she learned about herself. Now that Kiki has two kids who are also performers, the show ends with her feeling sure of herself and ready to make the future she wants for them.

601:43046.showDesc:
Spookily accurate (if bonkers) audience readings, songs & 70s team games. The Singing Psychic reveals the songs in your heart. Edinburgh & Adelaide Fringe sell-out show from the UK. Best Comedy Show Finalist (MontrealFringe) "A gloriously entertaining night out”(FemaleArts) 'Can work a crowd like no other' (GrumpyGayCritic) ★★★★

Playwright: Marysia Trembecka
Cast: Marysia Trembecka
Designers: Martin Butterworth

https://www.singingpsychic.tv marysiatrembecka.com | @TheSingingPsychic, @marysiatrembecka | X: @MarysiaT | YT: TheSingingPsychic

Do you possess hidden psychic powers waiting to be unveiled? Prepare to discover your inner mystic—direct from London England, The Singing Psychic promises an evening brimming with glittering 70s-style games, toe-tapping tunes and prizes. The Singing Psychic Game Show has captivated audiences worldwide with its blend of comedy and uncanny accuracy. From revealing the melodies in your heart to dispensing relationship advice, probing life's deepest questions and inspiring audiences, The Singing Psychic leaves no stone unturned in her quest to entertain and enlighten. The biggest attraction is her spookily accurate (if utterly bonkers) audience readings. Theatregoers can expect a whirlwind of entertainment with a line-up of fabulous team games including psychic Jenga, psychic bingo, audience readings, freeze dance, for‘tunes’ and more. Each show is a unique experience fuelled by audience interaction and improvised, heart-warming hilarity. Whether arriving solo or with friends, there's a place for everyone in The Singing Psychic's realm of fun and mystery. As The Singing Psychic says “I have just two rules – one is that what I reveal in the room stays in the room, and the other is that I am ALWAYS right.” The Singing Psychic Game Show is brilliantly bananas. It’s an hour of fun that builds on three years of sell-out Edinburgh and Adelaide Fringe shows and four and five star Edinburgh Fringe reviews; a Funny Women Best Show nomination, two Montreal Fringe nominations (Prix de Creativite, Outstanding Comedy Show); a European tour; and a special appearance at the official BRIT Awards after- party. The Singing Psychic is also a TEDx speaker.

601:43720.showDesc:
Juno nominee and last year’s holdover Shirley Gnome returns with a new direction. After accidentally creating a viral kids’ song on TikTok, the R-rated singer tries to cash in with a children’s music hour. Spoiler: it doesn’t go well. ★★★★★ “a bona fide genius” -Edmonton Journal. NOT SUITABLE FOR KIDS.

Playwright: Shirley Gnome, Becky Johnson
Cast: Shirley Gnome, children who wish to remain anonymous

Shirley Gnome is a singer who has spent sixteen years performing songs about adult topics. Even though she’s been doing this for a long time, she’s never had a big hit. That changes when a video of her at a music festival, looking a bit "out of it", gets posted on TikTok by a teenager. The video quickly goes viral. A popular TikTok creator named DJ BBN0$e turns the video into a song that becomes a huge hit with kids — like the next “Baby Shark.” Suddenly, Shirley finds herself famous with a new, younger audience. She decides to go along with it to make more money. She’s invited to perform at an elite private school, hoping this new direction will finally pay off. But things quickly fall apart. The kids hate the songs, and her lyrics raise disturbing questions that no child (or parent) is ready for. Through this experience, Shirley starts to reflect on her fears about the world, the future, and the kind of planet we’re leaving behind. What starts as a cash grab gig turns into something deeper — a search for hope. Let’s be clear: this is not a real kids' show. It’s satire. Please do not bring your children. Seriously.

Karin Fekko weaves tales of hilarity and tragedy through 17 years of keeping her MS a secret while partying, dating, and trying to land her dream job. This show is about invisible disability, internalized ableism, and big-time denial. Can we laugh about it? Karin thinks so. Join her as she marks 20 years since her MS diagnosis.

Website/Socials: @karinfekko

Multiple Neurosis is a storytelling show about a disease I have called Multiple Sclerosis. I tell stories about having a disability that is invisible. For example, when I was blind in one eye, other people did not notice. I talk about hiding my disability for 17 years, when going to parties, going on dates, and going to work. The show is about me trying to feel ok about having a disability, and not being ashamed. I have had Multiple Sclerosis for 20 years, and I finally tell people about it, instead of hiding it. I balance the serious parts of the show with fun and comedy.

601:43047.showDesc:
Spookily accurate (if bonkers) audience readings, songs & 70s team games. The Singing Psychic reveals the songs in your heart. Edinburgh & Adelaide Fringe sell-out show from the UK. Best Comedy Show Finalist (MontrealFringe) "A gloriously entertaining night out”(FemaleArts) 'Can work a crowd like no other' (GrumpyGayCritic) ★★★★

Playwright: Marysia Trembecka
Cast: Marysia Trembecka
Designers: Martin Butterworth

https://www.singingpsychic.tv marysiatrembecka.com | @TheSingingPsychic, @marysiatrembecka | X: @MarysiaT | YT: TheSingingPsychic

Do you possess hidden psychic powers waiting to be unveiled? Prepare to discover your inner mystic—direct from London England, The Singing Psychic promises an evening brimming with glittering 70s-style games, toe-tapping tunes and prizes. The Singing Psychic Game Show has captivated audiences worldwide with its blend of comedy and uncanny accuracy. From revealing the melodies in your heart to dispensing relationship advice, probing life's deepest questions and inspiring audiences, The Singing Psychic leaves no stone unturned in her quest to entertain and enlighten. The biggest attraction is her spookily accurate (if utterly bonkers) audience readings. Theatregoers can expect a whirlwind of entertainment with a line-up of fabulous team games including psychic Jenga, psychic bingo, audience readings, freeze dance, for‘tunes’ and more. Each show is a unique experience fuelled by audience interaction and improvised, heart-warming hilarity. Whether arriving solo or with friends, there's a place for everyone in The Singing Psychic's realm of fun and mystery. As The Singing Psychic says “I have just two rules – one is that what I reveal in the room stays in the room, and the other is that I am ALWAYS right.” The Singing Psychic Game Show is brilliantly bananas. It’s an hour of fun that builds on three years of sell-out Edinburgh and Adelaide Fringe shows and four and five star Edinburgh Fringe reviews; a Funny Women Best Show nomination, two Montreal Fringe nominations (Prix de Creativite, Outstanding Comedy Show); a European tour; and a special appearance at the official BRIT Awards after- party. The Singing Psychic is also a TEDx speaker.

601:43721.showName: SHIRLEY GNOME: Children, You Can't Beat 'Em! (this is not a kid's show)
601:43721.showDesc:
Juno nominee and last year’s holdover Shirley Gnome returns with a new direction. After accidentally creating a viral kids’ song on TikTok, the R-rated singer tries to cash in with a children’s music hour. Spoiler: it doesn’t go well. ★★★★★ “a bona fide genius” -Edmonton Journal. NOT SUITABLE FOR KIDS.

Playwright: Shirley Gnome, Becky Johnson
Cast: Shirley Gnome, children who wish to remain anonymous

Shirley Gnome is a singer who has spent sixteen years performing songs about adult topics. Even though she’s been doing this for a long time, she’s never had a big hit. That changes when a video of her at a music festival, looking a bit "out of it", gets posted on TikTok by a teenager. The video quickly goes viral. A popular TikTok creator named DJ BBN0$e turns the video into a song that becomes a huge hit with kids — like the next “Baby Shark.” Suddenly, Shirley finds herself famous with a new, younger audience. She decides to go along with it to make more money. She’s invited to perform at an elite private school, hoping this new direction will finally pay off. But things quickly fall apart. The kids hate the songs, and her lyrics raise disturbing questions that no child (or parent) is ready for. Through this experience, Shirley starts to reflect on her fears about the world, the future, and the kind of planet we’re leaving behind. What starts as a cash grab gig turns into something deeper — a search for hope. Let’s be clear: this is not a real kids' show. It’s satire. Please do not bring your children. Seriously.

Satire

Multiple Neurosis is a storytelling show about a disease I have called Multiple Sclerosis. I tell stories about having a disability that is invisible. For example, when I was blind in one eye, other people did not notice. I talk about hiding my disability for 17 years, when going to parties, going on dates, and going to work. The show is about me trying to feel ok about having a disability, and not being ashamed. I have had Multiple Sclerosis for 20 years, and I finally tell people about it, instead of hiding it. Playwright: Danielle Dresden & Kiki Moritsugu
Director: Francisco Torres
Cast: Kiki Moritsugu
Designers: Lyn Pilch (Choreography) Lizzie Haller (Music Direction)
Stage Manager: Ingrid Klottke
Accompianist: Mya Rosario

https://FB: Kiki Moritsugu | IG: @kiki_moritsugu

Kiki Moritsugu has a solo show where she talks about her strong connection with her mum, Jo Hutchings, who was a performer. In the show, Kiki sings, dances, and tells stories. She explains how growing up with a mum who was so bold and different helped her figure out who she is. Kiki is mixed race (part Japanese-Canadian and part English-Canadian), and she shares behind-the-scenes moments from her own job. She talks about how hard it was to find her own voice when other people had ideas about who she should be. The songs in the show are like a soundtrack to Kiki's life, helping us understand how she learned about herself. Now that Kiki has two kids who are also performers, the show ends with her feeling sure of herself and ready to make the future she wants for them.

Music/Musical Theatre

https://theatrefreeradical.wordpress.com

Orphan Well is a dark Alberta ghost story about Katie, a woman who seals old oil wells. While working at the site of a religious leader accused of abuse, she starts hearing voices underground. The experience brings up painful memories from her past. As she searches for the truth, Katie struggles to tell what’s real and avoid falling under the preacher’s influence. Inspired by real events, the show explores abuse of power, environmental damage, and the lasting effects of patriarchy.

Playwright: jem rolls

A man walks on stage and talks for an hour. He uses his voice, face, and arms a lot. What he says and does are short performance poems. There are about twelve in the show, and each one is different. Some are funny, some silly, and some clever. Two try to sound beautiful. One is a short story. One is about the environment and the future. One is fast and intense. Jem says nice things about Canada. In the last piece, the audience becomes God. Then the show ends, and everyone leaves.

Fresh off a hit run at the Toronto Fringe, Mathew's Big Broadway Bash brings powerhouse vocals, heartfelt storytelling, and surprise sing-a-longs. Big voice, bare feet, and all heart. "A unique Fringe treat."- Ross Steve, Front Mezz Junkies

Playwright: Mathew Mac Lean
Director: Mathew Mac Lean
Cast: Mathew Mac Lean

https://torontocabaretproductions.com

Mathew’s Big Broadway Bash is a fun and feel-good cabaret show filled with songs from your favourite musicals. It’s not a big Broadway show with big money — it’s a small, heartwarming show made with love, creativity, and a lot of passion. You’ll hear songs from shows like Grease, Cats, Mamma Mia!, The Little Mermaid, Pippin, and The Sound of Music. This is more than just a show — it’s a celebration! We sing, we laugh, and we welcome the audience to join the fun. Whether you sing along quietly or imagine yourself on stage, this night is for you. This cabaret is about community, joy, and making musical theatre more accessible for everyone. It’s bold, a little scrappy (this is Fringe!), and full of heart.
